_meta.description: Hand-written (query -> relevant product_ids) cases for the retrieval eval harness (docs/ShopTalk_Plan.md Phase 3 / PROJECT_REPORT.md §3). Primary metrics: Precision@K and MRR. Recall@K/NDCG are reported as approximate -- a sampled set like this can't give exhaustive or graded relevance labels across the full catalog.
_meta.scope: Drawn from the 200-product BLIP-2 dev-captioned sample (data/kaggle_process/products_enriched_blip2-opt-2.7b.parquet), itself a uniform random draw from the 39,733-product subsample -- so every relevant_item_id here is also a valid item in the full catalog. This keeps the set forward-compatible: when the full ~40K corpus is captioned and indexed (docs/PROJECT_REPORT.md §3.3 'dev-scale now, full-scale later'), these same cases can be re-run as-is. Full-scale indexing may surface additional plausible matches outside this list; that's expected and is exactly why Recall@K is reported as approximate, not exact.
_meta.themes: Queries are themed to the ACTUAL catalog mix (home/furniture decor, footwear, jewelry, electronics accessories, grocery, personal care) per the Phase-1 catalog-mix audit -- not generic apparel examples that would be sparsely represented in ABO.

categories.easy: Query phrasing closely mirrors the product name/type -- tests baseline lexical+semantic recall.
categories.attribute: Query names a specific color/material/type combination where the corpus contains multiple same-type items in DIFFERENT colors/materials -- the structured-pre-filter correctness cases ('a green chair never returns the brown/gray/black ones').
categories.hard: Naturalistic shopper phrasing that requires inference, synonym matching, or has more than one plausible relevant item -- tests whether semantic similarity (and the visual caption) carries the query past a literal keyword match.

_meta.eval_integrity_note: Written by hand against real catalog listings (not generated from any synthetic query template) -- the condition docs/ShopTalk_Plan.md §2.3 names for a fine-tuning comparison to mean anything later: golden queries must be naturalistic and distribution-disjoint from whatever template generates synthetic training queries in Phase 4.

cases.query: a wooden coffee table with a glass top
a small wooden side table for a living room corner
cases.note: two plausible end tables in the sample, both pine

cases.query: a grey area rug for the living room
cases.note: filter check: corpus also has a blue jute rug and a multi-colored striped rug

cases.query: a blue striped jute rug
a brown leather recliner chair
cases.note: filter check: a second recliner exists but in gray fabric/metal, not leather

cases.query: a green velvet accent chair
cases.note: filter check: corpus has chairs in brown, gray, black, beige -- only this one is green

cases.query: a black mesh-back office desk chair
a wooden folding chair
a square charcoal grey upholstered ottoman
a stone-colored chaise lounge for the living room
cases.note: 'stone' here is the listed material/finish, not a literal color word -- tests whether the model bridges that

cases.query: a blue hardside carry-on suitcase with a TSA lock
a matching luggage set with wheels for a family trip
cases.note: two multi-piece luggage sets in the sample

cases.query: black leather oxford dress shoes for men
navy blue sneakers for men
cases.note: two distinct navy men's sneakers in the sample

cases.query: red and black women's running shoes
brown shearling-lined ankle boots for women
white leather sandals with a wooden sole
light blue gladiator sandals
cases.note: listed color is 'None' -- only the visual caption ('light blue leather') carries this attribute, a direct test of caption-enriched value

cases.query: a ring with a blue topaz stone
a sterling silver necklace with a dog pendant
a diamond engagement ring
cases.note: both are cubic-zirconia 'engagement ring' style pieces a shopper would call a diamond ring

cases.query: gold stud earrings with a blue gemstone
silver heart-shaped drop earrings
a phone case with a duck on it
cases.note: distinctive visual motif named only in the caption, not the product title

cases.query: a phone case with panda bears on it
cases.note: distinctive visual motif named only in the caption, not the product title

cases.query: a phone case with a floral print
cases.note: two distinct floral-pattern phone cases in the sample

cases.query: a glittery pink phone case
a USB hub with multiple ports and a power adapter
an adapter to connect a laptop to a wired ethernet network
a black external hard drive for a laptop
pink in-ear headphones with a built-in microphone
a stainless steel flatware set that serves eight people
a pre-seasoned cast iron skillet
a set of wine glasses
a humidifier for a bedroom
a heavy-duty surge-protector power strip with mounting brackets
a large dog crate with two doors
a booster seat to keep a small dog secure in the car
cases.note: 'pet booster seat' phrased the way a shopper, not the catalog, would describe it

cases.query: a camping tent that sleeps four people
a green pop-up gazebo with side panels for the backyard
a wall-mounted coat rack with hooks
a chrome wall sconce light fixture with a glass shade
a matte black bathroom vanity light fixture
cases.note: filter check: corpus also has a chrome wall sconce -- same product_type, different color/finish

cases.query: a small pink ceramic planter
a set of green plastic storage containers with lids
a yellow binder for filing documents
a spiderman-themed comforter for a kid's bed
a duvet set with a leaf print
cases.note: listed color/material are both 'None' -- only the caption ('comforter ... blue leaves') names the pattern

cases.query: organic whole wheat bread with a touch of honey
a bag of mixed nuts as a snack
earl grey tea bags
a vanilla-scented soy wax candle
a pink faux-leather clutch bag with a chain strap
a tan leather tote-style handbag
women's leather ankle boots with a block heel
cases.note: listed color is 'None'; only the caption ('black ankle boot with a wooden heel') confirms the silhouette
